sections.paragraph:
ἀνατυλίσσω (τυλίσσω ‘twist’; cp. ἐντυλίσσω) 1 aor. ἀνετύλιξα (lit. ‘unroll’, βίβλια Lucian, Ind. 16) fig., think over or call to mind again τί (Lucian, Nig. 7 τ. λόγους, οὓς τότε ἤκουσα συναγείρων καὶ πρὸς ἐμαυτὸν ἀνατυλίττων) τὰ ἀπʼ ἀρχῆς γενόμενα 1 Cl 31:1.—DELG s.v. τύλη.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

aor. aor. = aorist

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 74
